subject: Computer Networking
title: Hardware Firewalls
description: A fast breakdown of Hardware Firewalls for builders.

hook.text: A hardware firewall sits at your network's edge like a bouncer. It decides who gets in.

buildup.text: It inspects every packet. Rules allow or block traffic based on IP, port, and protocol.

discovery.text: Next-gen firewalls inspect app content, detect malware, and identify users — not just IPs.

twist.text: Stateful inspection tracks active connections. It tells replies apart from unsolicited attacks.

climax.text: Palo Alto, Fortinet, and Cisco dominate. A single appliance can inspect 100+ Gbps of traffic.

punchline.text: A hardware firewall filters at wire speed. Your first line of defense.

quiz.question: What does stateful inspection track?
options: Active connections to distinguish replies from unsolicited packets
The physical location of each connected device
The temperature of each network port
